title: Cryopreservation of Mucosal Biopsies
author: Sean M. Hughes
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p5adq2e

steps.title: Prepare cryopreservation medium (10% dimethylsulfoxide in fetal bovine serum)
steps.instruction: Prepare cryopreservation medium (10% dimethylsulfoxide in fetal bovine serum). undetermined, fetal bovine serum undetermined, dimethylsulfoxide

steps.title: Aliquot 0
steps.instruction: Aliquot 0.2 mL cryopreservation medium into each cryovial. Larger volumes may be used with multiple biopsies. E.g. use 1 mL with 5-10 biopsies.

steps.title: Place one or more biopsies in each cryovial
steps.instruction: Place one or more biopsies in each cryovial. Ensure that the biopsies are completely covered with cryopreservation medium. Add more to cover if necessary.

steps.title: Close cryovial, place in a Mr
steps.instruction: Close cryovial, place in a Mr. Frosty, and freeze to -80C overnight. Any controlled rate cooling device that yields a decrease in temperature of 1C per minute can be used in place of a Mr. Frosty.

steps.title: For storage, place samples in a...
steps.instruction: For storage, place samples in a liquid nitrogen freezer until needed.

steps.title: Chill cryopreservation medium at 4C for at least 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Chill cryopreservation medium at 4C for at least 30 minutes. 00:30:00

steps.title: Put 5mL of cell culture medium...
steps.instruction: Put 5mL of cell culture medium of interest into a plate or well.

steps.title: Remove the cryovials from the liquid...
steps.instruction: Remove the cryovials from the liquid nitrogen freezer, but keep them on liquid nitrogen in a pan or other device for carrying liquid nitrogen until ready to thaw.

steps.title: Transfer cryovials to a 37C water...
steps.instruction: Transfer cryovials to a 37C water bath and agitate until thawed.

steps.title: Transfer biopsies with forceps into 5...
steps.instruction: Transfer biopsies with forceps into 5 mL of room temperature culture medium.

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00
